name: Rangi
description: Rangi, also known as Ranginui, is the Sky Father in Māori mythology. He is a primordial being who, along with Papa, the Earth Mother, formed the foundation of the universe. Initially, Rangi and Papa were locked in a tight embrace, their children trapped in the darkness between them. This oppressive darkness spurred their children, the gods, to seek separation. After many attempts, Tāne Mahuta, god of the forests, successfully forced his parents apart, pushing Rangi upwards to become the sky and allowing light to flood the world. Rangi's tears are said to be the rain, a constant reminder of his separation from Papa. He represents the heavens, the realm of light, and the source of knowledge and spiritual power. His separation from Papa is a central theme in Māori creation narratives, symbolizing the emergence of the world from darkness and the beginning of life. Rangi's influence is felt in the weather, the stars, and the connection between the earthly and spiritual realms. He is often invoked in prayers and ceremonies, representing the ancestral lineage and the ultimate source of being. He is a powerful and revered figure, embodying the sky's vastness and the mysteries of the cosmos. His separation from Papa also birthed the different realms of existence, with the gods each taking responsibility over different aspects of the world. Rangi's enduring sadness and the constant rain are a poignant reminder of the sacrifice made to bring light and life to the world.
subtitle: Sky Father, Source of Light and Rain

keyMyths.title: The Separation of Rangi and Papa
keyMyths.description: Rangi and Papa were locked in a tight embrace, trapping their children in darkness. Tāne Mahuta pushed them apart, creating the world of light.

keyMyths.title: Rangi's Tears
keyMyths.description: After being separated from Papa, Rangi weeps constantly, his tears falling as rain upon the earth. This rain sustains life and connects the sky and earth.
keyMyths.source: Orbell, The Illustrated Encyclopedia of Māori Myth and Legend

keyMyths.title: The Children's Struggle
keyMyths.description: The children of Rangi and Papa struggled in the darkness, seeking a way to bring light to the world. Their collective efforts led to the separation.
keyMyths.source: Reed, Māori Myths and Legendary Tales

sources.author: Sir George Grey
sources.description: A classic collection of Māori myths and legends, providing foundational accounts of Rangi and Papa.
sources.text: A classic collection of Māori myths and legends, providing foundational accounts of Rangi and Papa.
